title: BLS CPI-U — U.S. City Average, All Items (Annual Average) + Inflation Rate
description: National annual-average Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U), U.S. city average, all items, not seasonally adjusted (BLS series CUUR0000SA0, 1982-84=100). Carries the verbatim BLS annual-average index plus a computed year-over-year inflation rate and a deflator that converts a nominal dollar amount of a given year into latest-year dollars. Use for putting Massachusetts DDS examiner pay and SSA backlog dollars into real (inflation-adjusted) terms.

columns.name: year
columns.description: Calendar year of the annual-average index.

columns.name: annual_avg_index
columns.description: BLS annual-average CPI-U index (M13 annual average), U.S. city average, all items, NSA, 1982-84=100. Verbatim BLS.

columns.name: inflation_rate_pct
columns.description: Computed year-over-year percent change of annual_avg_index vs. the prior year. Blank if the prior year is unavailable.

columns.name: deflator_to_latest
columns.description: Computed: latest-year annual_avg_index divided by this year's index. Multiply a nominal dollar amount of this year by the deflator to express it in latest-year dollars.

source.title: CPI-U, U.S. City Average, All Items (CUUR0000SA0)

update_cadence: Annual (BLS publishes annual averages each January)
notes: The annual_avg_index column is verbatim BLS (the M13 annual-average rows pulled via the BLS Public Data API v2 by scripts/pull_bls_cpi.py); inflation_rate_pct and deflator_to_latest are computed in-repo. inflation_rate_pct is the standard year-over-year percent change of the annual average. deflator_to_latest uses the latest available year as the base (latest-year deflator = 1.0). Worked example: a VDE step paid $60,000 nominal in 2020 is worth $60,000 x deflator_to_latest[2020] in latest-year dollars, so pay-scale dollars from different years can be compared in real terms. Any year lacking a published annual average is left blank, never interpolated.
